CheckTitle: Security group restricts FTP access from the internet
Description: Checks that FTP TCP ports 20 and 21 are not exposed to unrestricted IPv4 (`0.0.0.0/0`) or IPv6 (`::/0`) sources.
Risk: FTP can expose credentials and data in clear text; public access enables brute force, interception, and unauthorized file transfer.

Code.Other: Inspect inbound rules in the ECS console. Locate effective Accept rules from `0.0.0.0/0` or `::/0` using TCP or ALL whose actual port range contains FTP ports `20` or `21`. Restrict the source or range, or revoke the exact rule by SecurityGroupRuleId. Revocation using protocol/range/source must exactly match the existing rule.

Recommendation.Text: Do not expose TCP ports 20 or 21 to `0.0.0.0/0` or `::/0`.
